 {"id":"bd-1lqa","title":"Convert pool mutation/acquire methods from expect() to map_err Result propagation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:58:54.666794749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:53:24.733406937Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T23:53:24.733275302Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lqa","depends_on_id":"bd-34nl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:33:12Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lqa","depends_on_id":"bd-3uhb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:33:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-1lqa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What

Convert all pool mutation and connection-acquisition methods that use `.expect("pool lock poisoned")` to return `Result<T, Error>` or `Outcome<T, Error>`.

## Where

File: crates/sqlmodel-pool/src/lib.rs

## Affected Methods (~13 call sites)

These are in the critical path — connection acquisition, creation, closing:
1. `acquire()` — already returns Outcome, just needs internal expect→map_err
2. `try_acquire()` — already returns Option/Result
3. `close()` — async, already returns Outcome
4. `create_connection()` — internal, multiple expect calls
5. `evict_idle()` — internal maintenance
6. `health_check()` — already returns Outcome

Most of these already return Result/Outcome so the change is internal — replace the expect() inside with map_err(). The signature stays the same.

## Implementation Pattern

For methods already returning Outcome:
```rust
// Before (inside acquire):
let mut inner = self.shared.inner.lock().expect("pool lock poisoned");
// After:
let mut inner = self.shared.inner.lock().map_err(|_| {
    Error::Pool("pool mutex poisoned during acquire()".into())
})?;
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- All remaining ~13 expect() calls converted
- Zero expect("pool lock poisoned") remaining in file
- All existing tests pass
- cargo clippy clean
","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:58:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1lv","title":"Implement PoolStatusDisplay dashboard renderable","description":"## Purpose
Create a visual dashboard renderable that displays connection pool status at a glance, showing pool utilization, health, and queue status.

## Background
Connection pools are critical infrastructure. Operators need to quickly assess:
- How many connections are active vs idle
- Whether the pool is under pressure (requests waiting)
- Health metrics (timeouts, errors)
- Configuration (min/max connections)

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/pool_status.rs

### Core Struct
PoolStatusDisplay struct holds:
- stats: PoolStats (from sqlmodel-pool)
- theme: Theme
- width: Option<usize>
- show_history: bool (show recent utilization trend)

### PoolStats (expected interface from sqlmodel-pool)
struct PoolStats {
    active_connections: u32,
    idle_connections: u32,
    waiting_requests: u32,
    max_connections: u32,
    min_connections: u32,
    total_acquired: u64,
    total_released: u64,
    avg_acquisition_time_ms: f64,
    pool_created_at: Instant,
}

### Visual Layout (Rich Mode)


### Health Status Logic
- HEALTHY: waiting=0, active < 80% of max
- BUSY: waiting=0, active >= 80% of max  
- DEGRADED: waiting > 0 but < max
- EXHAUSTED: waiting >= max or active = max for extended period

### Color Coding
- Healthy: green
- Busy: yellow
- Degraded: orange
- Exhausted: red

### Plain Text (Agent Mode)
Pool: 8/20 active (40%), 0 waiting, HEALTHY
  Active: 5, Idle: 3, Max: 20
  Avg acquire: 2.3ms, Uptime: 2h 15m

## API Design
PoolStatusDisplay::new(stats: &PoolStats)
  .theme(theme)
  .width(80)
  .show_history(true)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

## Verification Steps
1. Test with healthy pool (low utilization)
2. Test with busy pool (high utilization)
3. Test with exhausted pool (waiting > 0)
4. Verify bar chart renders correctly at various widths
5. Verify plain text output
6. Test uptime formatting
7. Test with zero stats (fresh pool)

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-pool for PoolStats interface
- rich_rust Panel, ProgressBar components
- Theme from this crate","acceptance_criteria":"PoolStatusDisplay shows active/idle/max connections
Display updates in real-time during pool activity
Display shows connection acquisition queue depth
Plain mode outputs status line format
All unit tests verify rendering
Performance tests confirm low overhead","notes":"PoolStatusDisplay dashboard fully implemented with PoolHealth enum, PoolStatsProvider trait, render_plain()/render_styled() methods, 23 tests passing, all quality gates pass","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:10.255536895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887703018Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:53:10.887618990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1q2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:10.284025904Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1lv","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:19:05.077873451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-1lv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_pool_status_creation - verify construction
2. test_pool_status_healthy - verify healthy state styling
3. test_pool_status_busy - verify busy state styling
4. test_pool_status_degraded - verify degraded state styling
5. test_pool_status_exhausted - verify exhausted state styling

 {"id":"bd-1m0","title":"Write comprehensive rustdoc for sqlmodel-console API","description":"## Purpose
Add thorough rustdoc documentation to all public types, traits, and functions in sqlmodel-console crate.

## Background
Rustdoc is the primary API reference for Rust libraries. Every public item needs:
- Purpose description
- Usage examples
- Parameter documentation
- Return value documentation
- Links to related items

## Implementation Details

### Documentation Scope
All public items in:
- lib.rs (crate overview, feature flags)
- console.rs (SqlModelConsole, OutputMode)
- theme.rs (Theme, color definitions)
- All renderables (ErrorPanel, QueryResultTable, etc.)
- Progress components
- Traits (ConsoleAware, etc.)

### Documentation Style

#### Module-level
//! # sqlmodel-console
//!
//! Rich terminal output for SQLModel Rust operations.
//!
//! ## Quick Start
//! (code example)
//!
//! ## Features
//! - Agent-safe output (auto-detects AI coding agents)
//! - Rich renderables (tables, panels, trees)
//! - Themeable colors
//!
//! ## Feature Flags
//! - console (default): Enable console output
//! - syntax: Enable SQL syntax highlighting

#### Struct Documentation
/// A styled error panel for displaying sqlmodel errors.
///
/// ErrorPanel renders errors with:
/// - Color-coded severity
/// - Error context and location
/// - Suggestions when available
///
/// # Examples
///
/// Basic usage:
/// (code example)
///
/// With custom theme:
/// (code example)
///
/// # Agent Compatibility
/// In plain mode, renders as: ERROR: message (code)
pub struct ErrorPanel { ... }

#### Method Documentation
/// Create a new error panel from an Error.
///
/// # Arguments
/// * error - The sqlmodel error to display
///
/// # Returns
/// A new ErrorPanel ready for rendering
///
/// # Example
/// (code example)
pub fn new(error: &Error) -> Self { ... }

### Cross-References
Use intra-doc links:
/// See also: [Theme::error_color]
/// Related: [QueryResultTable] for displaying results

### Feature-Gated Documentation
#[cfg_attr(doc, doc = "Requires feature: syntax")]
#[cfg(feature = "syntax")]
pub struct DdlDisplay { ... }

### Examples in Docs
All examples should:
- Compile (cargo test --doc)
- Be minimal but complete
- Show both rich and plain output where relevant

## Verification Steps
1. cargo doc builds without warnings
2. cargo test --doc passes
3. All public items have documentation
4. Examples are runnable
5. Cross-references resolve
6. Feature flags documented

## Dependencies
- All console APIs finalized
- Code examples working","acceptance_criteria":"All public types have rustdoc comments
All public functions have rustdoc comments with examples
Module-level documentation explains purpose
Examples in docs compile and run (doctests)
cargo doc generates without warnings
Documentation follows Rust API guidelines","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:16:58.274332388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692485588Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:56:17.692361477Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2e8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1m0","depends_on_id":"bd-2sh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1n7","title":"Query Building: Advanced SQL Features","description":"## Overview

Implement ALL advanced query building features from SQLAlchemy.

## Set Operations
- union(*selects) - UNION
- union_all(*selects) - UNION ALL  
- intersect(*selects) - INTERSECT
- intersect_all(*selects) - INTERSECT ALL
- except_(*selects) - EXCEPT
- except_all(*selects) - EXCEPT ALL

## Subqueries and CTEs
- alias(name=None) - Alias for subquery
- lateral(...) - Lateral subquery
- exists(select) - EXISTS clause
- cte(name, recursive=False) - Common Table Expressions

## Window Functions
- over(element, partition_by=None, order_by=None, range_=None, rows=None)
- within_group(element, *order_by)
- funcfilter(func, *criterion)

## Advanced Expressions
- extract(field, expr) - Extract date/time field
- type_coerce(expression, type_) - Type coercion
- tuple_(*clauses, types=None) - Create tuple
- lambda_stmt(lambda_func) - Lazy-compiled statement
- bindparam(key, value=None, type_=None) - Parameter binding
- values(*multiinsert_clauses) - VALUES clause

## Label and Collation
- label(name, element, type_=None) - Column label/alias
- collate(expression, collation) - Collation
- try_cast(expression, type_) - Safe cast (returns NULL on failure)

## Current Status

Basic expressions implemented. Missing:
- All set operations (UNION, INTERSECT, EXCEPT)
- CTEs (Common Table Expressions)
- Window functions (OVER, WITHIN_GROUP)
- Lateral subqueries
- Advanced type coercion","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:59:55.455588844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:26:22.260443668Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:26:22.260311972Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1n7","depends_on_id":"bd-162","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:33:12Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ng","title":"Create MigrationStatus panel for migration tracking","description":"## Purpose
Implement a renderable that displays migration status, showing applied vs pending migrations with timestamps, checksums, and visual indicators.

## Background
Migration management is critical for database schema evolution. Users need to see:
- Which migrations have been applied (with timestamps)
- Which migrations are pending
- Migration checksums for verification
- Visual distinction between states (applied/pending/failed)
- Direction indicators (up/down migrations)

## Implementation Details

### File Location
crates/sqlmodel-console/src/renderables/migration_status.rs

### Core Types
MigrationState enum: Applied, Pending, Failed, Skipped
MigrationRecord struct: version, name, state, applied_at, checksum, duration_ms
MigrationStatus struct: list of MigrationRecord, theme settings

### Visual Design (Rich Mode)
- Use Table with styled rows
- Applied migrations: green checkmark prefix, dim timestamp
- Pending migrations: yellow clock prefix
- Failed migrations: red X prefix, error message
- Columns: Status Icon, Version, Name, Applied At, Duration

### Plain Text Format (Agent Mode)
MIGRATION STATUS
===============
[OK] 001_create_users - Applied 2024-01-15 10:30:00 (45ms)
[OK] 002_add_email_index - Applied 2024-01-15 10:30:01 (12ms)
[PENDING] 003_add_posts_table
[PENDING] 004_add_comments_table

### API Design
MigrationStatus::new(records: Vec<MigrationRecord>)
  .theme(theme)
  .show_checksums(bool)
  .show_duration(bool)
  .render(width) / .render_plain()

### State-Based Styling
- Applied: theme.success_color() - typically green
- Pending: theme.warning_color() - typically yellow  
- Failed: theme.error_color() - typically red
- Skipped: theme.muted_color() - typically gray

## Usage Example
let status = MigrationStatus::new(vec![
    MigrationRecord {
        version: "001".into(),
        name: "create_users".into(),
        state: MigrationState::Applied,
        applied_at: Some(timestamp),
        checksum: Some("abc123".into()),
        duration_ms: Some(45),
    },
    MigrationRecord {
        version: "002".into(),
        name: "add_posts".into(),
        state: MigrationState::Pending,
        ..Default::default()
    },
]);
console.print_renderable(&status);

## Verification Steps
1. Test with mix of applied/pending migrations
2. Test with failed migration showing error
3. Verify plain text is machine-parseable
4. Test empty migration list
5. Verify timestamps format correctly
6. Test duration formatting (ms/s/min)
7. Verify checksum display when enabled

## Dependencies
- rich_rust Table component
- Theme from this crate
- DateTime formatting utilities","acceptance_criteria":"MigrationStatus panel shows migration name and version
Panel shows applied/pending status
Panel shows migration timestamp
Panel shows up/down SQL preview if available
Plain mode outputs migration status text
All unit tests verify rendering","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:11:10.368682498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.589975346Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:59:13.589918971Z","close_reason":"Implemented - parent phase closed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ng","depends_on_id":"bd-1rn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ng","depends_on_id":"bd-2g8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T16:57:33Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-1ng","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests to Implement

1. test_migration_status_creation - verify construction
2. test_migration_state_applied - verify applied styling
3. test_migration_state_pending - verify pending styling
4. test_migration_state_failed - verify failed styling
5. test_migration_render_plain - verify parseable text
6. test_migration_render_rich - verify table output
7. test_migration_timestamps - verify date formatting
8. test_migration_checksums - verify checksum display
9. test_migration_duration - verify timing display
10. test_migration_empty_list - edge case","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:27:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o5","title":"Implement RelatedMany<T> wrapper type for one-to-many relationships","description":"# Task: Implement RelatedMany<T> Wrapper Type for One-to-Many Relationships

## Context
RelatedMany<T> represents a one-to-many relationship (e.g., Team has many Heroes). Unlike Related<T> which holds a single object, RelatedMany<T> holds a collection.

## Design Decisions
1. **Vec-based storage**: Simple, efficient for typical use cases
2. **Optional pagination**: Support for cursor-based pagination on large collections
3. **Eager or lazy**: Can be populated at query time or deferred
4. **Mutation tracking**: Track adds/removes for cascade operations

## What to Implement

### 1. RelatedMany<T> Type
\`\`\`rust
use std::cell::OnceCell;

/// A collection of related objects (one-to-many).
#[derive(Debug)]
pub struct RelatedMany<T: Model> {
    /// The loaded objects (if fetched)
    loaded: OnceCell<Vec<T>>,
    /// Foreign key column on the related model
    fk_column: &'static str,
    /// Parent's primary key value
    parent_pk: Option<Value>,
    /// Whether the collection has been loaded
    load_attempted: bool,
    /// Pending additions (tracked for cascade insert)
    pending_add: Vec<T>,
    /// Pending removals (tracked for cascade delete/update)
    pending_remove: Vec<ObjectKey>,
}

impl<T: Model> RelatedMany<T> {
    /// Create an empty, unloaded collection.
    pub fn new(fk_column: &'static str) -> Self {
        Self {
            loaded: OnceCell::new(),
            fk_column,
            parent_pk: None,
            load_attempted: false,
            pending_add: Vec::new(),
            pending_remove: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Create with already-loaded objects.
    pub fn loaded(fk_column: &'static str, objects: Vec<T>) -> Self {
        let cell = OnceCell::new();
        let _ = cell.set(objects);
        Self {
            loaded: cell,
            fk_column,
            parent_pk: None,
            load_attempted: true,
            pending_add: Vec::new(),
            pending_remove: Vec::new(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Get the loaded objects (empty vec if not loaded).
    pub fn get(&self) -> &[T] {
        self.loaded.get().map(|v| v.as_slice()).unwrap_or(&[])
    }
    
    /// Check if the collection has been loaded.
    pub fn is_loaded(&self) -> bool {
        self.load_attempted
    }
    
    /// Get the count of loaded objects.
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.get().len()
    }
    
    /// Check if the collection is empty.
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
        self.get().is_empty()
    }
    
    /// Add an object to the collection (tracked for flush).
    pub fn add(&mut self, obj: T) {
        self.pending_add.push(obj);
    }
    
    /// Remove an object by key (tracked for flush).
    pub fn remove(&mut self, key: ObjectKey) {
        self.pending_remove.push(key);
    }
    
    /// Load the collection from the database.
    pub async fn load(&mut self, session: &mut Session, parent_pk: Value) -> Result<&[T]> {
        if self.load_attempted {
            return Ok(self.get());
        }
        
        self.parent_pk = Some(parent_pk.clone());
        self.load_attempted = true;
        
        let objects = select!(T)
            .filter(Expr::col(self.fk_column).eq(parent_pk))
            .all(&session.connection)
            .await?;
        
        let _ = self.loaded.set(objects);
        Ok(self.get())
    }
    
    /// Iterate over loaded objects.
    pub fn iter(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &T> {
        self.get().iter()
    }
}

impl<T: Model> IntoIterator for RelatedMany<T> {
    type Item = T;
    type IntoIter = std::vec::IntoIter<T>;
    
    fn into_iter(self) -> Self::IntoIter {
        self.loaded.into_inner().unwrap_or_default().into_iter()
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 2. Serde Support
\`\`\`rust
impl<T: Model + Serialize> Serialize for RelatedMany<T> {
    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, s: S) -> Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {
        self.get().serialize(s)
    }
}

impl<'de, T: Model + Deserialize<'de>> Deserialize<'de> for RelatedMany<T> {
    fn deserialize<D: Deserializer<'de>>(d: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error> {
        let objects = Vec::<T>::deserialize(d)?;
        Ok(RelatedMany::loaded("", objects)) // FK column set by macro
    }
}
\`\`\`

### 3. Batch Loading Support
\`\`\`rust
impl Session {
    /// Batch load one-to-many relationships for multiple parent objects.
    pub async fn load_many_collection<P, C>(
        &mut self,
        parents: &mut [P],
        accessor: impl Fn(&mut P) -> &mut RelatedMany<C>,
        parent_pk: impl Fn(&P) -> Value,
    ) -> Result<()>
    where
        P: Model,
        C: Model,
    {
        // Collect all parent PKs
        let pks: Vec<Value> = parents.iter()
            .map(|p| parent_pk(p))
            .collect();
        
        if pks.is_empty() { return Ok(()); }
        
        let fk_column = accessor(&mut parents[0]).fk_column;
        
        // Single query for all children
        let children = select!(C)
            .filter(Expr::col(fk_column).is_in(&pks))
            .all(&self.connection)
            .await?;
        
        // Group children by FK value
        let mut by_fk: HashMap<Value, Vec<C>> = HashMap::new();
        for child in children {
            let fk = child.get_field_value(fk_column)?;
            by_fk.entry(fk).or_default().push(child);
        }
        
        // Populate each RelatedMany
        for parent in parents {
            let pk = parent_pk(parent);
            let children = by_fk.remove(&pk).unwrap_or_default();
            let related = accessor(parent);
            let _ = related.loaded.set(children);
            related.load_attempted = true;
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Files to Modify
- \`crates/sqlmodel-core/src/relationship.rs\`
- \`crates/sqlmodel-session/src/lib.rs\`

## Dependencies
- RelationshipInfo struct (bd-1fz) - DONE
- Session struct (bd-qv5)

---

## Comprehensive Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

1. **Constructor Tests**
   - \`test_related_many_new_is_unloaded\`: new().is_loaded() == false
   - \`test_related_many_loaded_is_loaded\`: loaded([...]).is_loaded() == true
   - \`test_related_many_loaded_has_objects\`: Objects accessible via get()

2. **Accessor Tests**
   - \`test_get_unloaded_returns_empty\`: Unloaded returns &[]
   - \`test_get_loaded_returns_objects\`: Loaded returns actual objects
   - \`test_len_accurate\`: len() matches object count
   - \`test_is_empty_accurate\`: is_empty() correct for 0 and N objects
   - \`test_iter_yields_all\`: iter() visits all objects

3. **Mutation Tracking Tests**
   - \`test_add_tracks_pending\`: add(obj) adds to pending_add
   - \`test_remove_tracks_pending\`: remove(key) adds to pending_remove
   - \`test_pending_isolated_from_loaded\`: pending_add not in get()

4. **Load Tests**
   - \`test_load_fetches_from_db\`: load() executes query
   - \`test_load_caches_results\`: Second load() returns cached
   - \`test_load_uses_fk_column\`: Query filters by FK column
   - \`test_load_empty_collection\`: Parent with no children -> empty vec

5. **Iterator Tests**
   - \`test_into_iter_consumes\`: IntoIterator works
   - \`test_iter_by_ref\`: iter() borrows

6. **Serde Tests**
   - \`test_serialize_empty\`: [] serializes to []
   - \`test_serialize_with_objects\`: Objects serialized
   - \`test_deserialize_array\`: JSON array deserializes
   - \`test_roundtrip\`: serialize then deserialize preserves data

### Integration Tests

1. **With Real Database**
   - \`test_load_from_db_populates\`: Real SELECT populates collection
   - \`test_add_flush_inserts\`: add() + flush() inserts children
   - \`test_remove_flush_deletes\`: remove() + flush() deletes children

2. **Batch Loading**
   - \`test_batch_load_single_query\`: load_many_collection uses 1 query
   - \`test_batch_load_groups_correctly\`: Children assigned to right parents

### E2E Test Script

\`\`\`rust
/// E2E: One-to-many collection workflow
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_related_many_workflow() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Setup: Team with 5 Heroes
    setup_team_with_heroes(&pool, 1, 5).await;
    
    // Load team
    let team = session.get::<Team>(1).await.unwrap().unwrap();
    
    // Initially unloaded
    assert!(!team.heroes.is_loaded());
    tracing::debug!("Heroes not loaded yet");
    
    // Load the collection
    let heroes = team.heroes.load(&mut session, Value::Int(1)).await.unwrap();
    assert_eq!(heroes.len(), 5);
    tracing::info!(count = heroes.len(), "Loaded heroes");
    
    // Now cached
    assert!(team.heroes.is_loaded());
    assert_eq!(team.heroes.len(), 5);
}

/// E2E: Batch loading one-to-many
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_batch_load_collections() {
    let pool = setup_test_pool().await;
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    // Setup: 10 teams, each with 5 heroes = 50 heroes total
    for i in 1..=10 {
        setup_team_with_heroes(&pool, i, 5).await;
    }
    
    // Load all teams
    let mut teams = session.query::<Team>().all().await.unwrap();
    
    let query_count_before = get_query_count(&pool);
    
    // Batch load all hero collections
    session.load_many_collection(
        &mut teams,
        |t| &mut t.heroes,
        |t| Value::Int(t.id.unwrap()),
    ).await.unwrap();
    
    let query_count_after = get_query_count(&pool);
    
    // Should be single batched query
    assert_eq!(query_count_after - query_count_before, 1, "Should batch load");
    
    // All collections populated
    for team in &teams {
        assert!(team.heroes.is_loaded());
        assert_eq!(team.heroes.len(), 5);
    }
    
    tracing::info!("Batch loaded 50 heroes in 1 query");
}

/// E2E: Add to collection and flush
#[tokio::test]
async fn e2e_add_to_collection() {
    let mut session = Session::new(&pool).await.unwrap();
    
    let mut team = session.get::<Team>(1).await.unwrap().unwrap();
    
    // Add new hero to team
    let new_hero = Hero {
        id: None,
        name: "New Hero".into(),
        team_id: team.id,
    };
    team.heroes.add(new_hero);
    
    // Flush should insert the hero
    session.flush().await.unwrap();
    
    // Verify in DB
    // ...
}
\`\`\`

 {"id":"bd-21wb","title":"Fix lossy i64-to-f64 conversion — add precision-loss detection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:59:03.604635044Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:28:39.358087897Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:28:39.358018899Z","close_reason":"Addressed as part of bd-31ed: TryFrom<Value> for f64 now checks |i64| > 2^53, row.rs FromValue for f64 has same check, and to_f64_lossy() helper added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21wb","depends_on_id":"bd-9a25","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:33:12Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-21wb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## What

Add precision-loss detection to i64→f64 conversions in Value and Row.

## Where

Files:
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs, line 436 (TryFrom<Value> for f64)
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs (FromValue for f64)

## Details

i64 has 63 bits of precision. f64 has 53 bits of mantissa. For |i64| > 2^53 (9,007,199,254,740,992), the conversion silently rounds to the nearest representable f64.

Example: i64 value 9007199254740993 converts to f64 9007199254740992.0 — off by 1.

## Fix

```rust
Value::BigInt(v) => {
    if v.unsigned_abs() > (1u64 << 53) {
        return Err(Error::Type(TypeError {
            expected: "f64-representable integer",
            actual: format!("i64 {} exceeds f64 exact integer range (2^53)", v),
            column: None,
            rust_type: Some("f64"),
        }));
    }
    Ok(v as f64)
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- i64→f64 errors when |value| > 2^53
- Values within range convert correctly
- Tests for boundary values: 2^53, 2^53+1, -(2^53), -(2^53+1)
","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:59:04Z"},{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-21wb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## REFINEMENT: Opt-In Lossy vs Strict Conversion

### The User Experience Problem

Some users legitimately want lossy f32 conversions — for example, reading a DOUBLE column into an f32 field where they know the values are small. Making TryFrom strict by default is correct, but we should provide an escape hatch.

### Recommended Approach: Strict by Default, Lossy Helper

1. **TryFrom<Value> for f32** — STRICT (errors on precision loss). This is the default.

2. **Add a helper method on Value** — for users who accept lossy conversion:
```rust
impl Value {
    /// Convert to f32, allowing precision loss.
    /// Returns an error only for completely unrepresentable values (NaN, infinity).
    pub fn to_f32_lossy(&self) -> Result<f32, Error> { ... }

    /// Convert to f64, allowing precision loss for large integers.
    pub fn to_f64_lossy(&self) -> Result<f64, Error> { ... }
}
```

This gives users a clear choice:
- `value.try_into::<f32>()?` — strict, errors on precision loss
- `value.to_f32_lossy()?` — lenient, only errors on impossible conversions

### Scope Clarification: row.rs Must Match

The `FromValue` trait in row.rs is the OTHER path values take (database → Rust struct). It must use the same strict logic. Users who want lossy behavior in their model fields should use a wrapper type or handle it in their deserialize impl.

### Precision Loss Thresholds

For f32:
- i32 values: exact up to ±16,777,216 (2^24)
- i64 values: exact up to ±16,777,216 (same limit)
- f64 values: exact if the value is representable as f32 (check: `v as f32 as f64 == v`)

For f64:
- i64 values: exact up to ±9,007,199,254,740,992 (2^53)

### Testing Note

Test both the strict path (TryFrom) and the lossy helper (to_f32_lossy):
- Strict: verify errors at boundaries
- Lossy: verify success at same boundaries, errors only for infinity/NaN

2. crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs (FromValue for f64 — i64 path)
Both must check |value| > 2^53. Also add to_f64_lossy() helper on Value.","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:01Z"},{"id":75,"issue_id":"bd-21wb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION: Wrong refinement was pasted on this bead

The refinement comment above about f32/to_f32_lossy was copy-pasted from bd-31ed and doesn't belong here. The correct refinement for i64→f64 is:

### Strict by Default, Lossy Helper

1. **TryFrom<Value> for f64 (BigInt path)** — STRICT: errors when |i64| > 2^53
2. **Add `Value::to_f64_lossy()` helper** — allows precision loss, only errors on truly unrepresentable values

### Precision Threshold
- f64 mantissa: 53 bits → exact integers up to ±9,007,199,254,740,992 (2^53)
- i64 range: 64 bits → values beyond 2^53 silently round

### Scope (corrected)
- value.rs: TryFrom<Value> for f64, BigInt(v) arm — add |v| > 2^53 check
- row.rs: FromValue for f64, BigInt(v) arm — same check
- value.rs: Add `pub fn to_f64_lossy(&self) -> Result<f64, Error>` on Value
- The to_f32_lossy() helper belongs to bd-31ed, NOT this bead

 {"id":"bd-25i","title":"Implement OutputMode enum and detection logic","description":"# Implement OutputMode Enum and Detection Logic

## Task Description

Implement the core OutputMode enum and the detection function that determines
which mode to use based on environment variables and terminal state.

## File: src/mode.rs

```rust
//! Output mode detection for agent-safe console output.
//!
//! This module provides automatic detection of whether output should be
//! plain text (for AI agents and CI) or richly formatted (for humans).

use std::env;
use std::io::IsTerminal;

/// Output mode for console rendering.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum OutputMode {
    /// Plain text output, no ANSI codes. Machine-parseable.
    /// Used for: AI agents, CI, piped output, dumb terminals.
    Plain,
    
    /// Rich formatted output with colors, tables, panels.
    /// Used for: Interactive human terminal sessions.
    #[default]
    Rich,
    
    /// Structured JSON output for programmatic consumption.
    /// Used for: Tool integrations, scripting.
    Json,
}

impl OutputMode {
    /// Detect the appropriate output mode from environment.
    /// 
    /// Detection priority (first match wins):
    /// 1. SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 → Plain
    /// 2. SQLMODEL_JSON=1 → Json  
    /// 3. SQLMODEL_RICH=1 → Rich (overrides agent detection!)
    /// 4. NO_COLOR=1 → Plain (standard env var)
    /// 5. CI=true → Plain
    /// 6. TERM=dumb → Plain
    /// 7. Agent env vars → Plain (see is_agent_environment)
    /// 8. !is_tty(stdout) → Plain
    /// 9. Default → Rich
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // Explicit overrides (highest priority)
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_PLAIN") {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_JSON") {
            return Self::Json;
        }
        if env_is_truthy("SQLMODEL_RICH") {
            return Self::Rich;  // Force rich even for agents
        }
        
        // Standard "no color" convention
        if env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // CI environments
        if env_is_truthy("CI") {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Dumb terminal
        if env::var("TERM").map(|t| t == "dumb").unwrap_or(false) {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Agent detection
        if Self::is_agent_environment() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Not a TTY (piped, redirected)
        if !std::io::stdout().is_terminal() {
            return Self::Plain;
        }
        
        // Default: rich output for humans
        Self::Rich
    }
    
    /// Check if we're running in an AI coding agent environment.
    /// 
    /// Known agent environment variables:
    /// - CLAUDE_CODE: Claude Code CLI
    /// - CODEX_CLI: OpenAI Codex CLI
    /// - CURSOR_SESSION: Cursor IDE
    /// - AIDER_*: Aider coding assistant
    /// - AGENT_MODE: Generic agent marker
    /// - GITHUB_COPILOT_*: GitHub Copilot
    fn is_agent_environment() -> bool {
        let agent_markers = [
            "CLAUDE_CODE",
            "CODEX_CLI", 
            "CURSOR_SESSION",
            "AIDER_MODEL",
            "AIDER_REPO",
            "AGENT_MODE",
            "GITHUB_COPILOT",
            "CONTINUE_SESSION",  // Continue.dev
        ];
        
        agent_markers.iter().any(|var| env::var(var).is_ok())
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode should use ANSI codes.
    pub fn supports_ansi(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, Self::Rich)
    }
    
    /// Check if this mode should use structured format.
    pub fn is_structured(&self) -> bool {
        matches!(self, Self::Json)
    }
}

/// Check if an environment variable is set to a truthy value.
fn env_is_truthy(name: &str) -> bool {
    env::var(name)
        .map(|v| {
            let v = v.to_lowercase();
            v == "1" || v == "true" || v == "yes" || v == "on"
        })
        .unwrap_or(false)
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    use std::env;
    
    // Helper to run test with clean environment
    fn with_clean_env<F: FnOnce()>(f: F) {
        // Save and clear relevant vars
        let vars_to_clear = [
            "SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "SQLMODEL_JSON", "SQLMODEL_RICH",
            "NO_COLOR", "CI", "CLAUDE_CODE", "CODEX_CLI",
        ];
        let saved: Vec<_> = vars_to_clear.iter()
            .map(|&v| (v, env::var(v).ok()))
            .collect();
        
        for &var in &vars_to_clear {
            env::remove_var(var);
        }
        
        f();
        
        // Restore
        for (var, val) in saved {
            match val {
                Some(v) => env::set_var(var, v),
                None => env::remove_var(var),
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_explicit_plain_override() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("SQLMODEL_PLAIN", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
        });
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_agent_detection() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Plain);
        });
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn test_rich_override_beats_agent() {
        with_clean_env(|| {
            env::set_var("CLAUDE_CODE", "1");
            env::set_var("SQLMODEL_RICH", "1");
            assert_eq!(OutputMode::detect(), OutputMode::Rich);
        });
    }
}
```

## Key Implementation Details

### Why `is_agent_environment()` Is Separate
This function can be extended as new AI coding tools emerge. Having it as a 
separate function makes maintenance easier.

### Environment Variable Detection
We check for existence (`is_ok()`) rather than specific values for agent markers
because different tools may set them to different values. The presence of the
variable is the signal.

### Test Isolation
Tests manipulate environment variables, so they need careful isolation to avoid
affecting other tests or the system. The `with_clean_env` helper handles this.

## Verification

```bash
# Unit tests
cargo test -p sqlmodel-console mode::tests

# Manual testing
SQLMODEL_PLAIN=1 cargo run --example mode_check
CLAUDE_CODE=1 cargo run --example mode_check
cargo run --example mode_check  # Should be Rich if in terminal
```","acceptance_criteria":"OutputMode enum has Plain, Rich, Json variants
detect() function checks all environment variables in correct priority
Agent detection covers Claude Code, Codex CLI, Cursor, Aider, Copilot
CI environment detection works for major CI systems
All unit tests pass including edge cases
Documentation includes detection priority order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:04:27.538848161Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:06:28.347316301Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:06:28.347211444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mode","phase-2","rich-rust"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25i","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:04:27.571017250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
